notes: Frozen contract for the `source_contract` field set emitted by the 3.0 TypeScript CLI. Five CLI surfaces emit a `source_contract` (with structurally distinct field sets), and one (`state todo`) does not: prime, prime --context <capability>, state plan, state query --list-artifacts, and the per-capability bespoke `capability_context.context.<bespoke>.source_contract` nested under prime --context. The oracle pins (1) a shared `sourceContractPattern` that all five emissions follow at the structural level (always a top-level `source_contract: object`, always a boolean 'is raw artifact reads required' field, always a string policy field, often an array of caveats or fallbacks) and (2) per-command required keys, value types, and the small set of literal values that the CLI must emit. Field content that legitimately varies by caller (cli_fallback, confidence_caveats, caveats, fallback_commands, owns, deferred) is NOT pinned at the string-content level per AC4 — only the value type and shape are pinned. The npm-cli-surface.json oracle (packages/cli/test/cli/fixtures/oracle/npm-cli-surface.json) covers the cross-cutting npm exit-code matrix for these commands and is referenced, not duplicated. The invalid-input envelope oracle (packages/cli/test/cli/fixtures/oracle/invalid-input-envelope.json) is referenced where the source_contract consumer surfaces a bad input.

sharedSourceContractPattern.description: The structural shape every `source_contract` emission follows. The exact key NAMES vary by command (e.g. `raw_artifact_reads_required` vs. `raw_artifact_reads_required_for_discovery`), but every emission is a JSON object, always carries a boolean 'is raw reads required' field, always carries at least one string policy field, and usually carries at least one string-array field listing caveats, fallbacks, or owned state. Drift detection runs per command in `commands`; this shared pattern is the second-level guard that catches cross-cutting contract drift when the per-command oracles stay in lockstep.

rawArtifactReadsRequiredField.description: Whether the caller must read the raw artifact; false means the structured CLI output is sufficient.

rawArtifactPolicyField.description: A human-readable policy line that names the conditions under which raw artifact access is allowed.

caveatsOrFallbackField.description: An array of strings describing caveats (per-call), missing state, fallback commands, or owned state. The exact name varies; the value is always an array of strings whose content legitimately varies by caller (per AC4).

namesByCommand.state_plan: missing_state OR fallback OR summary_fields OR entry_fields
namesByCommand.prime_context_per_capability: caveats OR fallback_commands OR owns OR deferred

prime.description: Top-level `source_contract` for `agentera prime` (no `--context`). Holds the dashboard-level orientation field set plus a `capability_startup` sub-object that pins the boolean/string/array contract from the TODO enumeration. The `capability_context` key is always null on the bare `prime` path; it is filled in only on the `prime --context` path.

prime_context.description: Per-capability context for `agentera prime --context <capability>`. The top-level `payload.source_contract` is NOT emitted on the context path (the dashboard-level orientation fields are not applicable when a specific capability is requested — only `command`, `status`, and `capability_context` are at the top level). The per-capability bespoke contract lives at `payload.capability_context.context.<bespoke>.source_contract`, where `<bespoke>` is the per-capability context key (`orchestration_context` for orkestrera, `execution_context` for realisera, `evidence_context` for inspektera, `benchmark_context` for optimera, `closeout_context` for dokumentera). The five BESPOKE capabilities have a per-capability source_contract; the seven non-bespoke capabilities (hej, planera, resonera, inspirera, visionera, visualisera, profilera) only carry the shared `capability_context.context` shape with `first_invocation_read` and have no bespoke source_contract.

topLevelSourceContract.description: The top-level `source_contract` key is NOT emitted on the `prime --context` path — `buildPrimeCapabilityContextPayload` returns `{ command, status, capability_context }` and the dashboard-level `source_contract` (which lives on the bare `prime` path) is intentionally absent.

perCapabilitySourceContract.description: Per-capability bespoke source_contract at `payload.capability_context.context.<bespoke>.source_contract`. The required key set is shared across the five bespoke capabilities; the COMPLETENESS KEY and a few context-specific list fields vary by capability.

state_plan.description: Top-level `source_contract` for `agentera state plan`. PLAN-artifact-shaped contract that carries the persisted path, the complete flag, the included/complete state shapes, and the raw-access boundary policy.

state_todo.description: `agentera state todo` does NOT emit a `source_contract` on `--format json`. The required top-level keys are the standard state envelope (`command`, `status`, `entries`, `counts`, `source`, `filters`, `summary`) and that is owned by the npm-cli-surface.json oracle. The TODO enumeration in the live `source_contract` plan entry referenced the wrong field names; see TODO.md Resolved section (the freeze-fixtures entry was line 17 when task 4 was implemented, then moved to Resolved when the plan was archived) and plan.yaml surprises for the reconciliation. Documented here for completeness so the parity test does not falsely assert the presence of a `source_contract` key on this surface.

state_query_list_artifacts.description: Top-level `source_contract` for `agentera state query --list-artifacts`. The discovery-mode source contract has a distinct field set (no `complete_for_*` boolean; instead `raw_artifact_reads_required_for_discovery` and `normal_content_policy`) and an `allowed_raw_artifact_uses` list that names the seven allowed use cases. Field content (e.g. the list of allowed uses) is NOT pinned at the string-content level per AC4; only the value type is pinned.

fieldNameReconciliation.description: TODO.md's now-Resolved freeze-fixtures entry (originally on line 17 when task 4 was implemented, moved to Resolved when the plan was archived) enumerated source_contract fields as `complete_for_capability_startup`, `missing`, `cli_fallback`, `raw_artifact_reads_required`, `confidence_caveats`. The LIVE prime output keys are `capability_startup_complete` in the docs-summary sub-shape, `missing_state`, `cli_fallback`, `raw_artifact_reads_required`, `confidence_caveats`. The TODO enumerated `complete_for_capability_startup` (a key inside `capability_startup`) and `missing` (a truncation of `missing_state`); both were reconciled in TODO.md in the same commit per plan AC1. This oracle pins the LIVE keys: `complete_for_capability_startup`, `missing_state`, `cli_fallback`, `raw_artifact_reads_required`, `confidence_caveats`.
